9-6.notes: Rea's Win32 telecom example: automated mediator copies spec changes to XML, eliminating manual C++ header-file updates and the human-error accuracy loss they caused

16-5.notes: Proxy pattern (GoF–TRIZ mapping by Domb & Stamey): substituting a resource-heavy secure object with a lightweight configurable proxy changes the instantiation cost parameter without altering the interface

19-10.notes: Distributed microservices example cited by Mann: two-phase commit increases complexity while risking data loss; principles inferred from Mann's framework on distributed data integrity

5-2.notes: Flyweight pattern (GoF–TRIZ mapping by Domb & Stamey): shared intrinsic state is projected into an external pool (Another Dimension) to preserve execution speed while minimising runtime memory
